name: Type Erasure

laws: Generic type arguments are removed (or unified) after type checking
Runtime carries no (or limited) reified generic type information
Casts / bridges may be inserted by the compiler
x.encoding: Java generics (erased to bounds), C++ type-erased wrappers (std::any, std::function, std::shared_ptr deleter), C# in contrast reifies generics; Rust monomorphises (no erasure) but dyn Trait erases the concrete type.

def: Compiling away generic/concrete type information after type-checking, so the runtime works with a common representation rather than per-type code or reified type args.
problem: Provide generics/abstraction without per-instantiation code bloat or pervasive runtime type metadata.
mechanism: After static checking, the compiler discards the specific type parameters (Java) or hides a concrete type behind a uniform interface (C++ std::function, Rust dyn Trait). Behaviour goes through a shared representation or vtable rather than specialised code.
limits: Loses runtime type info (reflection limits, no `new T()`)
Pitfalls: arrays of generics, overload clashes (Java)
failures: Assuming generic type info exists at runtime (Java)
Heap/indirection overhead in hot paths from erased wrappers
whenNot: When reflection over type args is required (use reified generics, C#)
myths: "All generics are erased" — C# reifies; Rust monomorphises; only some systems erase
